definition: The union of Rudra (Shiva) and his consort (Shakti); also refers to a large canonical Tantric scripture from which the VBT is extracted.
The 'threefold' system of Kashmir Shaivism, emphasizing triads such as Shiva (the divine), Shakti (the energy), and Nara (the individual).
Essence, core, or quintessence of a teaching or state of consciousness.
That which has been heard; sacred revelation, oral transmission of scriptural teachings.
The 'mass of sounds'; the entire collection of letters/phonemes of the Sanskrit alphabet, representing the creative cosmic matrix.
The 'little mothers'; the powers of the phonemes of the alphabet which form the unrecognized basis of language and thoughts, binding or freeing the mind.
A part, digit (e.g., of the moon), aspect, or dynamic power/energy associated with a phoneme or state.
The ninefold form of Bhairava or the nine-syllable mantra representing the nine stages of cosmic structure.
The 'three-headed' form of Bhairava, representing the three aspects of consciousness (transcendent, intermediate, relative).
The triad of energies: Parā (transcendent), Parāparā (mixed/intermediate), and Aparā (immanent/relative). Also corresponds to will (icchā), knowledge (jñāna), and action (kriyā).
Subtle inner mystical sound resonance; the primary sound vibration of the universe.
A concentrated point of light, energy, or consciousness; the condensed focus-drop in yoga.
The 'crescent moon'; a highly refined stage of sound resonance during the ascent of energy through the central channel.
The 'impeder' or blocker; a threshold stage in the ascent of sound energy near the third eye where force-based effort must dissolve into surrender.
Vowelless; a consonant sounded purely without vowel support, used in advanced mantra breath-retention practices.
The supreme, transcendent, undivided energy of consciousness.
The immanent, lower, relative energy manifesting as the physical world of diversity.
The intermediate energy that combines both transcendent and immanent aspects; the bridge between formless and form.
With parts (sa-kala); manifest, divided, or finite creation.
An essential principle or category of existence; the absolute Reality.
